content:
💡 snip | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enhance AI coding workflows by enabling visual communication, allowing AI agents to render diagrams and previews and users to annotate and provide feedback directly on these visuals.
✨ Key Features: • Visual communication layer for AI agents • Capture, annotate, and render diagrams/previews • Integration with AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
📖 Summary: Snip is a visual communication tool for AI agents, enabling users to capture, annotate, and render diagrams and previews directly within their AI coding workflows. It integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code to replace textual descriptions with visual outputs, facilitating a more intuitive review and iteration process.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 reclip | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: Downloading videos and audio from a wide variety of websites through a user-friendly web interface.
✨ Key Features: • Download videos from 1000+ supported sites (via yt-dlp) • MP4 video or MP3 audio extraction • Quality/resolution picker • Bulk downloads • Automatic URL deduplication
📖 Summary: ReClip is a self-hosted, open-source media downloader that allows users to download videos and audio from over 1000 websites. It features a clean web UI, supports MP4 and MP3 formats, and offers quality selection and bulk download capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 home-gallery | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a private, self-hosted, and feature-rich web gallery for users to organize, browse, and discover their personal photo and video collections, with an emphasis on user privacy and mobile accessibility.
✨ Key Features: • Self-hosted • Open-source • Web gallery • Mobile-friendly • Tagging
📖 Summary: HomeGallery is a self-hosted, open-source web gallery designed for personal photo and video collections. It offers features like tagging, mobile-friendliness, and AI-powered image and face discovery to help users browse and rediscover their media archives privately.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 summarize | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To quickly extract the essential information from web pages, documents, and media content through an accessible CLI and browser extension.
✨ Key Features: • Chrome Side Panel chat with streaming agent and history • Video slide extraction with OCR and transcript cards for YouTube and video files • Media-aware summarization (auto-detects audio/video vs. page content) • Support for multiple coding CLI backends (Codex, Claude, Gemini, etc.)
📖 Summary: Summarize is a versatile tool that provides concise summaries from various sources including URLs, files, and media like YouTube videos. It offers both a command-line interface (CLI) and a Chrome Side Panel extension for convenient access.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
